_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: 3U CubeSat primary frame, Al 7075, PC/104 standoffs, deployable rail guide grooves (CDS spec)
physics_paths: Load path: Corner posts distribute loads from solar panels and antennas.
Thermal path: Heat dissipation through aluminum fins.
Motion path: Rail guides allow deployment.
Electrical/Data path: Standoffs ensure electrical isolation between boards.
Human/Service access path: Access panels for maintenance.
integration_opportunities: Enhance thermal management with additional fins.
Integrate deployable rail guides for payload deployment.
verification_focus: Ensure proper clearance around access panels.

generated_parts.label: Antenna Deployer x 2

The deployer must integrate with a radio board and provide a clear RF signal path to the antenna.
Ensure replaceable module paths, cable harness visibility, and proper access features for maintenance.

risk.desc: Potential thermal issues due to proximity to sun-exposed surfaces.
risk.mit: Implement thermal management solutions such as heat sinks or insulation.

risk.desc: Risk of RF interference from nearby electronic components.
risk.mit: Ensure proper shielding and spacing between RF components and other electronics.

risk.desc: Deployment failure if burn-wire release does not function correctly.
risk.mit: Include redundant release mechanisms and test extensively before launch.

risk.desc: Risk of mechanical failure due to improper strain relief on cables.
risk.mit: Ensure all cables have appropriate strain relief and follow recommended bend radii.

risk.desc: Risk of incorrect mating due to lack of keying on connectors.
risk.mit: Add keying or labeling to connectors to prevent mis-mating.

physics_paths.description: Deploy force from motor to antenna
RF signal from radio board to antenna
Thermal management from exterior to deploy mechanism
Mechanical motion from motor to latching tab
Electrical power from battery to motor

integration_opportunities.details: Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown.
Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels.
Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover.

integration_opportunities.opportunity: Harness Strain Relief and Bend Radius
integration_opportunities.details: Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges.
Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating.
Motor power lines and sensor/encoder signal lines run in separate channels or are spaced/crossed at right angles, with the separation visible in routing geometry.
Each electronics module states its grounding scheme (star point, chassis bond, isolated) and signal returns route alongside their signals.
PCBs mount on standoffs with keep-out clearance to walls and moving parts, connector edges face access openings, and mounting holes match standard board patterns.

verification_focus.details: Critical joints need visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence and torque_Nm or preload intent.
Make part_tree children and geometry_ops mutually traceable by id/name.

manufacturing_strategy.process: CNC machining for Al 7075 parts, PCB fabrication for FR4 sensor window, and CNC for CFRP baffle shield

manufacturing_strategy.support: Support structures must be removed after machining

requirements: EO payload optics bench, thermal stability, optical baffles, focal plane interface, lens mounts
Removable payload module: optics bench, focal plane, thermal baffles, inspection window
Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown
Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels
Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover
Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges
Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating
Rails, PC/104 standoffs, payload bay, and board stack clearances are visible
Avionics, battery, antenna, and payload modules have removable trays or access panels
Design is an educational structural/electrical mockup and avoids flight qualification claims
Structure, power, avionics, comms, and payload are decomposed with simple mass/volume/power budget notes per module that sum consistently
